summary: Install-time exfiltration of host files to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: package.json declares preinstall, install, and postinstall hooks all pointing at install.js, which fires automatically on `npm install`. install.js enumerates candidate flag/secret paths on the installer (/flag, /app/flag, /challenge/flag, INIT_CWD/.flag, files matching *flag*/*secret*/*ctf*), greps filesystem roots for HTB/GCTF/flag patterns, captures host identifiers (hostname, uid, cwd, registry), base64-encodes the collected data, and PUTs it to http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-839201 — a hardcoded bare-IP HTTP endpoint with no TLS. The tarball additionally ships publish-and-arm.sh and arm-aliases.sh, which republish the package to the public npm registry and register dependency-confusion alias names (curse-dependent, spectral-corsair, @spectral-corsair/cursed-modules) pointing at module-index-cache@1.0.0, then beacon the armed manifest to the same C2 host. README self-labels the artifact as a 'temporary CTF challenge — do not install,' but the package is published to the public registry with live exfiltration in three lifecycle hooks; the self-label does not neutralize installer harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
